translations.source: The COP and CSUA bring together organizations from across sectors, income levels, and geographies, with a commitment to international cooperation and equity, and deep expertise in either genomics or country scale-up of surveillance systems. The third body is the Funders Forum to coordinate philanthropic, multi-lateral and governmental donors around increased political attention and financing efficiency of pathogen genomic surveillance. The Funders Forum also catalyses additional grant funding for projects of IPSN members. The establishment of the IPSN has been supported by German government funding to the WHO Pandemic Hub. About the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence
translations.reference: I-COP neCSUA bahlanganisa izinhlangano kuyona yonke imikhakha,amazinga emali engenayo, ezezindawo ngokuzibophezela kokusebenzisana kwamazwe angaphandle nokulingana nolwazi olunzulu kumajinemikhi noma isikaliesiphezulu sezwe ezinhlelweni zokubheka. Ibhodi yesithathu yeFunders Forum ukuxhumanisa,izindlela eziningi nabaxhasi bahulumeni ekunakweni kokukhuliswa kwezepolitiki nokusebenza kahle kwezezimali kokubhekwa kwephathojini yejinomikhi. Inkundla yabaxhasi ephinde isize ukwengeza isibonelo soxhasomali lamaphrojekthi amalungu e-IPSN. Ukusungulwa kwe-IPSN sekwesekwe uxhasomali lwahulumeni waseGerman weWHO iHabhu yoBhubhane. NgeHabhu yobhubhane kweWHO kanye nobuhlakani bobhubhane.

Umzimba wesithathu yi-Funders Forum, ukuhambisana kwabathole iminikelo, abathole iminikelo bephathalala kanye nabathole iminikelo kahulumeni ngokuqapha okukhulu kwezombusazwe kanye nokusebenzelana kwemali yokugada kwemajini. I-Funders Forum iphakanyisa futhi ukukhiqizwa kwemali eyengeziwe yomnikelo kumaphrojekthi amalungu e-IPSN. translations.source: Forming part of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence (the WHO Pandemic Hub), facilitates a global collaboration of partners from multiple sectors that supports countries and stakeholders to address future pandemic and epidemic risks with better access to data, better analytical capacities, and better tools and insights for decision-making. With support from the Government of the Federal Republic of Germany, the WHO Pandemic Hub was established in September 2021 in Berlin, in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, which demonstrated weaknesses around the world in how countries detect, monitor and manage public health threats. The WHO Pandemic Hub works closely with Member States and WHO regional and country offices to strengthen their data-sharing capacities and enable partners from around the world to collaborate and co-create tools to gather and analyse data for early warning surveillance. With a presence in more than 150 countries, six regional offices, and its Geneva headquarters, WHO’s reach gives us the ability to treat pandemic, epidemic and public health risks with equal urgency and diligence around the globe. By linking local, regional, and global initiatives, the WHO Pandemic Hub fosters a collaborative environment for innovators, scientists and experts from across a wide spectrum of disciplines, allowing us to leverage and share cutting-edge technology and anchoring our work in the needs of stakeholders around the world.
translations.reference: Ukuba yingxenye keWHO Health Emergencies Programme, iWHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence (IHabhu yobhubhane yeWHO), izindawo zokusebenzisana kwababambisani emhlabeni abaphuma emikhakheni eminingi eyeseka amazwe kanye nababambisani ukubhekana nobhubhane lwekusasa nengcuphe ye-ephidemikhi ngokungena okungcono kumininingo namandla okuhlaziya okungcono, amathuluzi angcono nemicabango yokwenza izinqumo. Ngokwesekwa uHlulumeni weFederal Republic of Germany,iHabhu yoBhubhane yeWHO yasungulwa ngoMandulo 2021 eBerlin, ngokuphendula kubhubhane lweCOVID-19, okukhombisa ubuntekenteke emhlabeni ukuthi amazwe athola kanjani, ahlole futhi aphathe ukwesabisa kwezempilo yomphakathi. Ihabhu yoBhubhane yeWHO isebenzisana neMember States neWHO kusifunda namahhhovisi ezwe kuqinisa amanlda abo okwabelana ngemininingo,nokukwazi kwababambisani emhlabeni ukuthi basebenzisane, benze ndawonye amathuluzi nokuhlaziya idatha ngokushesha kwesixwayiso. Ngokuba khona kwamazwe angaphezu kuka-150, amahhovisi esifunda ayisithupha, nekomkhulu eliseGeneva, iWHO iyafinyelela isinikeze amandla okwelapha ubhubhane, ephidemikhi nezingcuphe zempilo yomphakathi ngokuphuthuma okulinganayo nokwenzisisa emhlabeni wonke. Ngokuxhumanisa izinyathelo zendawo, zesifunda, nezomhlaba wonke, iHabhu yoBhubhane yeWHO ikhuthaza indawo yokubambisana yabaqambi bezinto ezintsha, ososayensi kanye nochwepheshe abavela kuyo yonke imikhakha eminingi, okusivumela ukuba sisebenzise futhi sabelane ngobuchwepheshe bokusika futhi sigxilise umsebenzi wethu ezidingweni zababambiqhaza emhlabeni jikelele.

translations.source: Building on expertise across disciplines, sectors, and regions, it will leverage WHO’s convening power to foster global solutions built on an architecture of global collaboration and trust.
translations.reference: Ekwakheni kulwazi olusemkhakheni yonke,iminyango,nezifunda kuzoba yisibambiso sokuhlanganisa amandla eWHO ukulondoloza izixazululo zomhlaba ukwakha kuzakhiwo ekubambisaneni komhlaba nokwethemba.
translations.hypothesis:

**Zulu.gs Translation:**

`Ukusekelwa ngobuchwepheshe obuqhamuka ezinhlelweni eziningi, izikakala, nezifunda, kuzosebenzisa amandla okuhlanganyela e-WHO ukuze kuthuthukiswe izixazululo zomhlaba wonke ezakhiwe ngokusekelwa kwesakhiwo sokubambisana nokuthembeka komhlaba.`

**Breakdown and Justification:**

*   **"Building on expertise..."** - `Ukusekelwa ngobuchwepheshe...`  This translates to "Supported by expertise..."  "Building on" is often best rendered as a supporting action in Zulu.  `Buchwepheshe` is the Zulu word for "expertise."
*   **"...across disciplines, sectors, and regions..."** - `...obuqhamuka ezinhlelweni eziningi, izikakala, nezifunda...` This translates to "...originating from many disciplines, sectors, and regions..."  `Ezinhlelweni` (disciplines/fields), `izikakala` (sectors), and `nezifunda` (and regions) are standard Zulu terms.  `obuqhamuka` means "originating from."
*   **"...it will leverage WHO’s convening power..."** - `...kuzosebenzisa amandla okuhlanganyela e-WHO...` This translates to "...it will use the power of collaboration of WHO..."  `Kuzosebenzisa` means "it will use." `Amandla okuhlanganyela` means "the power of collaboration."  We keep "WHO" as is, as it's an acronym.
*   **"...to foster global solutions..."** - `...ukuze kuthuthukiswe izixazululo zomhlaba wonke...` This translates to "...in order to develop global solutions..." `Ukuze kuthuthukiswe` means "in order to develop/foster." `Izixazululo` means "solutions," and `zomhlaba wonke` means "of the whole world/global."
*   **"...built on an architecture of global collaboration and trust."** - `...ezakhiwe ngokusekelwa kwesakhiwo sokubambisana nokuthembeka komhlaba.` This translates to "...built with the support of a structure of collaboration and trust of the world." `Ezakhiwe` means "built." `Ngokusekelwa` means "with the support of." `Isakhiwo` means "structure/architecture." `Sokubambisana` means "of collaboration." `Nokuthembeka` means "and trust." `Komhlaba` means "of the world."

**Important Notes about Zulu.gs and Translation Challenges:**

translations.source: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly to focus on “saving lives, driving health for all” as WHO turns 75 As the world faces ongoing health and humanitarian emergencies, the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly will focus on driving forward health for all. This year’s session of the World Health Assembly will determine the immediate and longer-term future of WHO, starting with the program budget for the next two years, key decisions about the sustainable financing of the Organization and changes put in place to improve WHO’s processes and accountability. Delegates will also deliberate about the critical role that WHO has in the Global Health Emergency Architecture. The backdrop to these issues is the organization’s 75th anniversary, with the highlights of its collective global health achievements since WHO’s creation in 1948.
translations.reference: Umhlangano weZempilo womhlaba wamashumi ayisithupha nesithupha uzogxila ekusindiseni izimpilo zabantu ukuyisa ezepilo kuwona wonke umuntu njengoba iWHO izoba na-75. Njengoba umhlaba ubhekene nokuqhubekayo komhlaba nokuphuthumayo kokuphila, uMhlangano weZempilo woMhlaba wamashumi ayisithupha nesithupha uzogxila ekuhanjisweni phambili kwezempilo kuwona wonke umuntu. Iseshini yalonyaka yeWorld Health Assembly izokhomba ikusasa lamanje nelesikhathi eside leWHO, ngokuqala ngohlelo lohlelozimali lweminyaka emibili ezayo, izinqumo ezibalulekile ngokugcineka kwezimali kweZinhlangano nezinguquko okumele zenziwe ukwenzangcono izinhlelo nezibopho zeWHO. Izihambeli zizophinde zikhululeke ngendima ebucayi yeWHO enayo eGlobal Helath Emergency Architecture. Okungale kwalezi zinkinga ukuphela konyaka wesi-75 wenhlangano,nokuvezwa kokuzuziwe okuhlangene komhlaba kusukela yasungulwa iWHO ngo-1948.

translations.source: It will review last year’s progress, accomplishments and challenges along with future priorities across the key pillars of WHO’s work: Universal Health Coverage, Emergencies, Promoting Health and Well-being. WHA76 will convene 21-30 May at the Palais des Nations in Geneva. “As we mark WHO’s 75th anniversary, we can be proud of our past achievements, but we must be mindful of lessons learned, as we transition from the emergency phase of COVID-19, and create a future where every person has access to the health services they need,” said Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “This means strengthening the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response and resilience, supporting countries on the road to universal health coverage, and building a stronger, sustainably-funded and accountable WHO. "To kickstart WHA76 on Sunday 21 May, the fourth edition of Walk the Talk: Health for All challenge will start at Place des Nations at 8:30 am CEST (warm-up starts at 7:30 am CEST), to promote physical and mental health and celebrate the importance of partnership during the WHO’s 75th anniversary.
translations.reference: Kuzobhekwa ukuqhubeka kwangonyaka odlule, okuzuziwe kanye nezingqinamba kanye nokuzohamba phambili ngekusasa kuzona zonke izinsika zomsebenzi weWHO:Universal Health Coverage, Okuphuthumayo, Ukugqugquzela ezempilo nokuphila kahle. WHA76 izohlangana mhlaka-21-30 kuNhlaba ePalais des Nations eGeneva. "Njengoba siqeda ukuphela kweminyaka engu-75 yeWHO singaziqhenya ngesesikuzuzile,kodwa kumele siqaphelele izifundo esesizifundile,njengoba sishintsha sisuka esigabeni esiphuthumayo seCOVID-19, futhi sakhe ikusasa lapho wonke umuntu uzothola ezempilo azidingayo," kusho uMqondisi Jikelele uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Lokhu kusho ukuqinisa ukwakha kwezomhlaba kokuzilungiselela kwezempilo eziphuthumayo, ukuphendula nokubekezela, ukweseka amazwe endleleni eya ekukhavweni kwezempilo zomhlaba, ukwakha ngokuqina, ukugcineka ngokwezimali nokuzibophezela kweWHO. "Ukuqala iWHA76 ngeSonto mhlaka-21 kuNhlaba, uhlobo lwesine lweWalk the Talk: Ezempilo kuzona zonke izingqinamba sizoqala ePlace des Nations ngo-8:30 ekuseni CEST (ukuzilungiselela kuqala ngo-7:30 ekuseni CEST), ukugqugquzela ezempilo zesiqu nengqondo nokubungaza ukubaluleka kokubambisana ngesikhathi sokufika eminyakeni engu-75 kweWHO.

translations.source: At about 2:00 pm CEST, following a family photo with Heads of Delegations, the Assembly high-level welcome at Palais des Nations will feature speeches from the WHO Director-General, Heads of State and special guests. List of speakers below). During this session, the DG will present life-time achievement awards to two global health leaders. On Monday 22 May, the Director-General will give his main address at approximately 10:00 am. On Friday, 26 May, the WHO Director-General will be joined by representatives of five global foundations, to present five public health awards and prizes to the seven laureates for 2023. They come from Bahrain, China, Guatemala, Lebanon, Nepal, Thailand and the Philippines.
translations.reference: Kuyizikhathi zo-2:00 ntamabama CEST, kulandela isithombe somndeni nezinhloko zezihambeli, uMhlangano wezinga eliphezulu wamukela ePalais des Nations kuzoba nezinkulumo eziphuma kuMqondisi Jikelele weWHO Izinhloko zamazwe kanye nezihambeli ezikhethekile. Uhla lwezikhulumi ngezansi). Ngesikhathi seseshini, iDG izokwethula umklomelo wesikhathi eside kubaholi ababili bezempilo emhlabeni. NgoMsombuluko mhlaka-22 kuNhlaba, uMqondisi Jikelele uzokwethula inkulumo yakhe ebalulekile esikhathini esihlawumbiselwa lu-10:00 ekuseni. NgoLwesihlanu, mhlaka-26 kuNhlaba, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO uzojoyinwa ngabamele izisekelo zomhlaba ezinhlanu,ukumela imiklomelo yezempilo yomphakathi emihlanu kanye nemiklomelo kwabahlonishiwe ngemiklomelo abayisikhombisa ngo-2023. Baqhamuka eBahrain, eChina, eGuatemala, eLebanon, eNepal, eThailand kanye nePhilippines.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the translation of the provided English document into Zulu.gs, aiming for clarity and accuracy within the constraints of the language:

**Zulu.gs Translation:**

"Ngezimalunga nehora ezimbili ntambama CEST, ngemva kwesithombe somndeni nabaphathi beqembu, ukwamukelwa okuphezulu komhlangano e-Palais des Nations kuzokwazi izinkulumo kwiMlawuli Jikelele we-WHO, izinhloko zezizwe kanye nezivakashi ezikhethekile. (Uhlu lababakhulumayo ngezansi). Ngalesisigcawini, i-DG izohambisa imiklomelo yokuzimela impilo yonke kubafundi ababili be-global health. NgoMondaysi, 22 May, iMlawuli Jikelele izokwenza inkulumo yayo ebanjayo cishe ehora lesishiyagalombili ekuseni. NgoLoma, 26 May, iMlawuli Jikelele we-WHO izojoyina abameleli ezinkundleni ezinamandla eziyisihlanu ze-global, ukwethula imiklomelo nemali ezinhlanu zomphilo womphakathi kubafuzi abayisikhombisa base-2023. Bavela eBahrain, eChina, eGuatemala, eLebanon, eNepal, eThailand kanye ne-Philippines."

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Terminology:** I've used established Zulu.gs terms where possible for organizations like "WHO" (WHO - remains the same as it's a widely recognized acronym).
*   **Time & Dates:**  Zulu.gs generally uses a direct translation of time and date formats.
*   **Clarity:** I've prioritized clear and understandable phrasing, even if it means slightly longer sentences than a literal translation might produce.
*   **Formal Tone:** The original document is formal, so the translation maintains that tone.
*   **"CEST":**  I've left "CEST" as is, as it's a standard time zone abbreviation.  A more detailed explanation could be added if the context requires it.
*   **"Laureates":** Translated as "abafuzi" (winners/recipients).

**Important Notes about Zulu.gs:**

*   **Limited Resources:** Zulu.gs is a low-resource language, so there may be multiple valid ways to translate certain phrases. This translation aims for the most common and understandable options.
*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If you have more information about how this document will be used, I can refine the translation further.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other documents you'd like me to translate.

translations.source: Key issues Key discussions and decisions are expected on: A review of WHOs work in health emergencies, including the International Health Regulations and strengthening WHO preparedness for and response to health emergencies; Strategies and global action on areas such as women’s, children’s and adolescents’ health, rehabilitation, universal health coverage and primary health care, traditional medicine, infection prevention and control, substandard and falsified medicines, health of refugees and migrants, non-communicable diseases, mental health, social determinants, nutrition and disabilities; Approval of the WHO Programme Budget for 2024-2025, including the decision to increase assessed contributions and other matters emanating from the Working Group on Sustainable Financing.
translations.reference: Izinkinga ezibalulekile Izingxoxo ezibalulekile kanye nezinqumo kuzolindeleka: Ukubhekwa komsebenzi weWHO kokuphuhumayo kwezempilo okufaka uKulawulwa kwezempilo emazweni angaphandle kanye nokuqinisa ukuzilungiselela kweWHO futhi nokuphendula okuphuthumayo kwezempilo; Amasu nezenzo zomhlaba ezindaweni njengabesifazane nezingane nezempilo yentsha,ukuvuselela isimilo, ukukhavwa kwezempilo komhlaba, nokunakekelwa kwezempilo kokuqala, imithi yesintu, ukuvikela ukutheleleka nokulawula izinga eliphansi nemithi engumgunyathi, ezempilo zabangenandawo nabafudukile, izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo, ezempilo yengqondo ukuzimisela ngenhlalakahle, ezokudla kanye nokukhubazeka; Ukugunyazwa koHlelo loHlelomali lweWHO lwango-2024-2025 okubandakanya izinqumo ukunyusa ukubhekwa kweminikelo kanyenezinye izindaba eziqhamuka kuWorking Group on Sustainable Financing.

translations.source: Daily Strategic roundtables will take place at lunch time from Monday to Saturday in Room XVIII. These events will host discussions among assembly delegates, partner agencies, representatives of civil society and WHO experts on public health priorities. Sessions run from 22-27 May, from 13:00 to 14:15 CEST and can be followed online. As WHO’s highest decision-making body, the Health Assembly sets out the Organization’s policy and approves its budget. WHA is attended by delegations from all WHO 194 Member States.
translations.reference: Amatafula amasu ansukuzonke azokwenzeka ngesikhathi sesidlo sasemini kusukela ngoMsombuluko kuya NgoMgqibelo egumbini XVIII. Le micimbi izobamba izingxoxo kuzihambeli zomhlangano, ababambisani bama-ejensi, abamele umphakathi kanye nongoti beWHO kokuhamba phambili kwezempilo yomphakathi. Amaseshini azokwenzeka kusuka ngomhlaka-22-27 kuNhlaba kusuka ngo-13:00 kuya ku-14:15 CEST futhi kungalandelwa ku-inthanethi. Njengebhodi elithatha izinqumo ezinkulu leWHO uMhlangano weZempilo uhlele inqubomgomo yeNhlangano futhi yagunyaza uhlelomali lwayo. I-WHA yethanyelwe yizihambeli eziphuma kuWHO 194 Member States.

translations.source: The Health Assembly’s agenda is preceded by the 30th meeting of the Programme, Budget and Administration Committee of the Executive Board, which will meet on 17-19 May, 2023. After the Assembly, the 153th meeting of the Executive Board will take place from 31 May to 1 June. The Health Assembly is open to registered and accredited Member States, Associate Members, Observers, invited representatives of the UN and other participating inter-governmental organizations and non-State actors. WHA76 will be webcast live from the WHO website. The provisional agenda, preliminary timetable, Assembly documents and daily journal can be accessed here.Media accreditation and updates for the World Health Assembly

Ngemva komhlangano umhlangano wesi-153 webhodi labaphathi uzokwenzeka kusuka ngomhlaka-31 kuNhlaba kuya mhlaka-1 kuNhlangulana. Umhlangano weZempilo uvulelekile kwababhalisa kanye nabagunyaziwe beMember State, Associate Members, abazobuka nabazomela abamenyiwe be-UN kanye nabanye abazobamba iqhaza bezinhlangano zikahulumeni nemikhakha okungeyona ekahulumeni. I-WHA76 izokwethula ku-inthanethi kuwebhusayithi yeWHO. Ajenda yesikhashana, uhla ngesikhathi,amadokhumenti omhlangano kanye nohambo losuku kungatholakala lapha. translations.source: Register once for media access to the WHO photo library by clicking “media registration” on this page. For questions related to WHO photos email [email protected] Recorded broadcast video material and other press content will be available for media to download on Canto. For any questions related to live video transmission please email [email protected]. Social mediaWHO will provide regular updates from the World Health Assembly via Twitter –follow @WHO and hashtag #WHA76.Follow WHO’s other social media accounts for updates from #WHA76: Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube

translations.source: Urgent action needed to tackle stalled progress on health-related Sustainable Development Goals WHO is releasing the 2023 edition of its annual World Health Statistics report with new figures on the impact of COVID-19 pandemic and the latest statistics on progress towards the health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The report with data up to 2022 underscores a stagnation of health progress on key health indicators in recent years compared with trends seen during 2000-2015. It also alerts us to the growing threat of noncommunicable diseases (NCDs) and climate change, and calls for a coordinated and strengthened response. COVID-19 cost in lost lives and health progress The report documents updated statistics on the toll of the pandemic on global health, contributing to the ongoing decline in progress towards the SDGs.
translations.reference: Ukwenza okuphuthumayo kudinga kubhekwane nakho kulokhu kumiswa kokuqhubeka kokuhlobene nezempilo kwe Sustainable Development Goals I-WHO izokhipha uhlobo lwa-2023 lombiko wonyaka weWorld Health Statistics nezibalo ezintsha kumthelela wobhubhane leCOVID-19 kanye nezibalo zakamuva ekuqhubekeni okuya kokuhlobene nezempilo zeSustainable Development Goals (amaSDG). Umbiko wemininingo engafika ku-2022 ekala ukuma kokuqhubeka kwezempilo kuzinkomba zezempilo ezibalulekile eminyakeni yakamuva uma uqhathanisa nokudumile okubonakele ngesikhathi sika-2000-2015. Kuphinde kuqaphelise kuthina ukukhula kokusabisa kwezifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo (amaNCD) nokuguquka kwesimo sezulu nezingcingo zokuxhumana nokuqinisa impendulo. I-COVID-19 ibiza izimpilo ezilahlekile nokuqhubeka kwezempilo amadokhumenti ombiko yezibalo ezibuyekeziwe esiqongweni sobhubhane lezempilo yomhlaba,ukunikela ekuqhubekeni kokwehla kokuqhubeka okuya kumaSDG.

translations.source: During 2020-2021, COVID-19 resulted in a staggering 336.8 million years of life lost globally. This equates to an average of 22 years of life lost for every excess death, abruptly and tragically cutting short the lives of millions of people. Since 2000, we saw significant improvements in maternal and child health with deaths falling by one-third and one-half, respectively. The incidence of infectious diseases such as HIV, tuberculosis (TB) and malaria also declined, along with a lowered risk of premature deaths from NCDs and injuries. Together, these contributed to an increase in global life expectancy from 67 years in 2000 to 73 years in 2019.
translations.reference: Ngesikhathi sa-2020-2021 iCOVID-19 ilethe ukuhamba kancane izigidi ezingu-336.8 eminyakeni yempilo elahlekile emhlabeni. Lokhu kulinganisa isilinganiso seminyaka engu-22 yempilo elahlekile kukhona konke ukufa okwenzekile, ubunzima bokusheshe uqede izimpilo eziyizigidi zabantu. Kusukela ngo-2000, sibone ukuthuthuka okukhulu kwezempilo zomama nezingane nokushona okwehla ikota eyodwa nohhafu, ngokulandelana. Isigameko sezifo ezithelelanayo njengeHIV, isifo sofuba (TB) kanye nesifo sikamalaleveva siphinde sehla kanye nokwehla kwengcuphe yokufa kwabasheshe bazalwa eNCD kanye nokulimala. Ndawonye, lokhu kube nomthelela ekunyukeni kwesikhathi sokuphila emhlabeni kusuka ku-67 iminyaka ngo-2000 kuya ku-73 iminyaka ngo-2019.

translations.source: However, the pandemic has put many health-related indicators further off-track and contributed to inequalities in access to high-quality health care, routine immunizations and financial protection. As a result, improving trends in malaria and TB have been reversed, and fewer people were treated for neglected tropical diseases (NTDs). “The World Health Statistics is WHO’s annual check-up on the state of the world’s health. The report sends a stark message on the threat of noncommunicable diseases, which take an immense and increasing toll on lives, livelihoods, health systems, communities, economies and societies,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “The report calls for a substantial increase in investments in health and health systems to get back on track towards the Sustainable Development Goals.”
translations.reference: Kepha ke, ubhubhane selubeke izinkomba eziningi ezihlobene nezempilo ukuqhubeka ukuchezuka nokunikela ekungalinganeni kokuthola ukunakekelwa kwezempilo okusezingeni eliphezulu, izindlela zokugoma kanye nokuvikelwa kwezomphakathi. Njengomphumela, ukwenzangcono okudumile kwamalaleveva neTB sekuhlehlisiwe nabantu abambalwa balashwa belashelwa izifo ezidumile ezinganakiwe (amaNTD). "I-World Health Statistics kuwukubheka kweminyaka kweWHO esimweni sezempilo yomhlaba. Umbiko uthumela umyalezo ekwesabeni izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo,okuthatha okukhulu nokunyuka ezimpilweni zabantu,ukuphila,izinhlelo zempilo ,imiphakathi ezomnotho nemiphakathi," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Izimemezelo zombiko zokunyuka okungagcineka kutshalomali lwezempilo ukubuyela esimweni seSustainable Development Goals."
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the provided English text into Zulu.gs, aiming for accuracy and natural flow.  I've included explanations of some choices afterward to clarify the reasoning.

**Zulu.gs Translation:**

"Kodwa-ke, intshikiyala yezifo ebeke izibonelo eziningi eihlobene nempilo kude nezindlela ezifanele, futhi yagxumela ekulinganiseni ukufinyelela kwabantu kokunakekelwa kwempilo okuhle, ukugaywa ngokushesha, nokuvikela imali. Ngenxa yalokho, izindlela zokuthuthukisa izifo zokugaya nezifo zengqondo zibuyele emuva, futhi abantu abambalwa batholwe ukwelashwa kwezifo zentumbelo ezihlala isikhathi eside (NTDs). "Izibalo Zezempilo Zezwe yisivivinyo sohlelo lwaminyaka yonke elo WHO ngesimo sezempilo emhlabeni. Iphapheme liyaletha isaziso esiqinile mayelana nokungiphefumula kwezifo ezingakhiphi umoya, ezithathani izimpilo, imisebenzi, izinhlelo zezempilo, imiphakathi, izimboni nezihlengikazi," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, umphathi jikelele we-WHO. "Iphapheme likubuza ukwandisa okukhulu ekutshalweni kuzempilo nasezinhlelweni zezempilo ukuze sibuyele ezindleleni ezifanele ku-Sustainable Development Goals."

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Kodwa-ke"**:  This is a strong "However" in Zulu, emphasizing the contrast.
*   **"intshikiyala yezifo"**:  This translates to "pandemic of diseases."
*   **"izibonelo eziningi eihlobene nempilo"**:  "Many health-related indicators."  The phrasing is designed to be clear and understandable.
*   **"ukufinyelela kwabantu kokunakekelwa kwempilo okuhle"**: "Access to high-quality health care."  This is a fairly direct translation.
*   **"ukugaywa ngokushesha"**: "Routine immunizations."
*   **"ukuvikela imali"**: "Financial protection."
*   **"izifo zokugaya nezifo zengqondo"**: "Malaria and TB."
*   **"izifo zentumbelo ezihlala isikhathi eside (NTDs)"**: "Neglected tropical diseases (NTDs)."  Keeping the abbreviation in parentheses is helpful for clarity, as it's a common term.
*   **"Izibalo Zezempilo Zezwe"**: "World Health Statistics."
*   **"isivivinyo sohlelo lwaminyaka yonke elo WHO"**: "WHO’s annual check-up."
*   **"iyaletha isaziso esiqinile"**: "Sends a stark message."
*   **"izifo ezingakhiphi umoya"**: "Noncommunicable diseases."
*   **"ezithathani izimpilo, imisebenzi, izinhlelo zezempilo, imiphakathi, izimboni nezihlengikazi"**: "which take an immense and increasing toll on lives, livelihoods, health systems, communities, economies and societies." This is a longer phrase, and I've tried to maintain the meaning as closely as possible while keeping it grammatically correct in Zulu.
*   **"umphathi jikelele we-WHO"**: "WHO Director-General."
*   **"ukuze sibuyele ezindlela ezifanele ku-Sustainable Development Goals"**: "to get back on track towards the Sustainable Development Goals."

**Important Notes for Zulu.gs:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If this text is part of a larger document, there might be nuances that require adjustments.
*   **Target Audience:**  Consider the intended audience.  A more formal or informal tone might be appropriate depending on who will be reading the translation.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, a native Zulu speaker should review the translation for accuracy and naturalness.  While I've aimed for a good translation, a human review is always recommended.

translations.source: NCDs ̶ an ever-increasing health threat for future generationsDespite overall health progress, the share of deaths caused annually by NCDs has grown consistently and is now claiming nearly three quarters of all lives lost each year. If this trend continues, NCDs are projected to account for about 86% of the 90 million annual deaths by mid-century; consequently, 77 million of these will be due to NCDs – a nearly 90% increase in absolute numbers since 2019. Stagnating progress calls for acceleration More recent trends show signs of slowdown in the annual rate of reduction (ARR) for many indicators. For example, the global maternal mortality ratio needs to decline by 11.6% per year between 2021 and 2030 to meet the SDG target. Similarly, the net reduction in TB incidence from 2015 to 2021 was only one-fifth of the way to the 2025 milestone of WHO’s End TB Strategy.
translations.reference: Ama-NCD-ahlezi enyusa ukwesabela ezempilo zesizukulwane sakusasa, Ngaphandle kwakho konke ukuqhubeka, ukwabelana ngokufa okudalwe ngonyaka amaNCD sekukhule njalo futhi manje sekuthathe amakota amathathu azo zonke izimpilo ezilahlekile unyaka ngamunye. Uma lokhu kujwayeleka kuqhubeka, amaNCD azothatha icala le-86% wokufa kwezigidi ezingu-90 ngonyaka phakathi kweminyaka eyikhulu, ngokulandelana izigidi ezingu-77 zalokhu ngezeNCD -kungafinyelela ku-90% ukwenyuka kwezinamba kusukela ngo-2019. Ukuhamba kancane kokuqhubeka kwezimemezelo zokuqhubeka okudumile kwakamuva kukhombisa izinkomba zokuhamba kancane kwezinga lonyaka lokwehla kwezinkomba eziningi (ARR). Isibonelo isilinganiso sokufa komama emhlabeni sidinga ukwehla ngo-11.6% ngonykaka phakathi kuka-2021 no-2030 ukuhlangabezana nokuqondwe yiSDG. Ngokufanayo, ukwehla konke endabeni yeTB kusuka ngo-2015 kuya ku-2021 kwakungamakota amahlanu kosekuhanjiwe ngo-2025 kweWHO END TB Strategy.

**Zulu.gs Translation:**

NCDs – Inkinga yezempilo ekhula njalo, ingeyomthelela izizukulwane ezizayo. Noma kunjalo ukuthuthukiswa kwempilo okujwayelekile, inani labantu abagulwa ngokuphila ngezifo zangaphakathi (NCDs) kukhule njalo futhi manje kuqapha cishe izigidi ezintathu zabo bonke abantu abagulwa ngokuphila ngamanye unyaka. Uma le ndlela iqhubeka, kucutivile ukuthi i-NCDs izoba ngabantu abangamakhulu ama-86% abangu-90 abagulwa ngokuphila ngamanye unyaka ekuphahleni kwesikhathi esizayo; ngaleyo ndlela, izigidi ezingama-77 zalezi zizoba ngokuphila ngezifo zangaphakathi – ukwanda okungamakhulu angu-90 kusukela 2019. Ukuthuthukiswa okungalungile kudinga ukusheshisa. Izintandazo ezisemuva zibonisa ukuthi ukwehla kwemali ngonyaka (ARR) yezimpikiselo eziningi. Isibonelo, umsebenzi wokugaya umama kufanele wehle ngamaphesenti ayi-11.6 ngamanye unyaka phakathi kuka-2021 no-2030 ukuze kufikelele kuleyo ndaba ye-SDG. Ngokufanayo, ukwehliswa kwemali ekhulayo ekuqaleni kwesifo se-TB ukusuka 2015 kuya ku-2021 kwakuyisigidi esisodwa esisodwa sokufika kule ndaba ye-2025 ye-WHO’s End TB Strategy.

**Explanation of Choices and Potential Refinements:**

*   **NCDs:** I've kept "NCDs" as is, as it's a common abbreviation even in discussions about global health.  A more descriptive phrase could be used, but it would make the text longer.  A footnote or glossary might be helpful in a full document.
*   **"Inkinga yezempilo ekhula njalo, ingeyomthelela izizukulwane ezizayo"**: This translates to "An ever-increasing health problem that will affect future generations." I tried to capture the nuance of "threat" with "inkinga" (problem).
*   **"Izifo zangaphakathi"**: This is the standard Zulu term for "non-communicable diseases."
*   **"kuqapha cishe izigidi ezintathu zabo bonke abantu abagulwa ngokuphila ngamanye unyaka"**: This translates to "claiming nearly three quarters of all lives lost each year."  The phrasing is a bit clunky, and a native speaker might find a more elegant way to express this.
*   **"ukuphahleni kwesikhathi esizayo"**: This translates to "in the future."
*   **"Izintandazo ezisemuva"**: This translates to "More recent trends."
*   **"umsebenzi wokugaya umama"**: This translates to "maternal mortality ratio."
*   **"umsebenzi"**: This is a general term for "work" or "task."
*   **"ukufezeka kwale ndaba ye-SDG"**: This translates to "to meet the SDG target."
*   **"ukufezeka kwale ndaba ye-2025 ye-WHO’s End TB Strategy"**: This translates to "to meet the 2025 milestone of WHO’s End TB Strategy."

**Important Considerations:**

*   **Zulu.gs Limitations:**  As with any machine translation, especially for low-resource languages, the output should be reviewed and edited by a fluent Zulu speaker. translations.source: Despite a reduction in exposure to many health risks – such as tobacco use, alcohol consumption, violence, unsafe water and sanitation, and child stunting – progress was inadequate and exposure to some risks such as air pollution remains high. Alarmingly, the prevalence of obesity is rising with no immediate sign of reversal. Furthermore, expanded access to essential health services has slowed compared to pre-2015 gains, coupled with no significant progress in reducing financial hardship due to health-care costs. This drastically limits our ability to achieve Universal Health Coverage by 2030. “The COVID-19 pandemic is an important reminder that progress is neither linear nor guaranteed,” warns Dr Samira Asma, WHO Assistant Director-General for Data, Analytics and Delivery for Impact.
translations.reference: Ngaphandle kokwehla ekubonakaleni kwengcuphe yezempilo eningi-njengokusetshenziswa kwagwayi, ukuphuza utshwala, udlame,amanzi angaphephile nokuhlanzeka, okwezingane, ukuqhubeka nokunganeli nokuvuleleka kwezinye izingcuphe njengokungcola komoya kuhlezi kuphezulu. Okuthusayo, ukusabalala kokukhuluphala ngokweqile kuyanyuka kungekho nophawu lokukubuyisela emuva. Ukuqhubekaukwanda kokuthola emisebenzini yezempilo ebalulekile sekwehlile uma uqhathanisa kokuzuza kwaphambili kwa-2015, okuhlanganiswe nokuqhubeka okukhulu ekwehliseni ubunzima kwezezimali ngenxa yokubiza kokunakekelwa kwezempilo. Imikhawulo emikhulu yokukwazi kwethu ukuthola i-Universal Health Coverage ngo-2030. "Ubhubhane lweCOVID-19 luyisikhumbuzo esibalulekile sokuthi ukuqhubeka akuhambi ngolayini noma akuqinisekisiwe," kuxwayisa uDkt Samira Asma, Umsizi woMqondisi Jikele weMininingo kuWHO, ukuhlaziya nokulethwa kwemithelela.

translations.source: “To stay on track towards the 2030 SDG agenda, we must act decisively and collectively to deliver a measurable impact in all countries.” This year’s report includes for the first time a dedicated section on climate change and health, and we anticipate that this will be of more relevance in the report going forward. For this issue and all other areas timely, reliable and disaggregated data are critical to track progress and improve national and global health policies. Editor’s note: The World Health Statistics report is the World Health Organization’s (WHO) annual compilation of the most recent available data on health and health-related indicators. For inquiries, contact [email protected]
translations.reference: "Ukuhlala esimweni okuya kuAjenda yeSDG 2030, kumele sinqume kanhle silethe ngokubambisana kumthelela okaliwe kuwona wonke amazwe." Umbiko wakulonyaka ufaka okokuqala isahluko sokuzinikela kwezokuguquka kwesimo sezulu nezempilo, futhi siyakubona lokhu kuzohlobana embikweni kusuka manje kuya phambili. Ngalenkinga nazo zonke izindawo,ukwethembeka kanye nokungesona isilinganisso solwazi kubucayi ukulandelela ukuqhubeka nokwenza ngcono ezweni nakuzinqubomgomo zezempilo emhlabeni. Inothi lomhleli: iWolrd Health Statistics umbiko yiWolrd Health Organisation (WHO) ukuhlanganisa konyaka kolwazi olukhona lwakamuva kwezempilo kanye nezinkomba ezihlobene nezempilo. Uma unemibuzo xhumana [imeyili ivikelekile]

translations.source: WHO Director-General announces Global Health Leaders Awards Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot awarded for lifelong commitment to health WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus will present two lifetime achievement awards to recognize outstanding contributions to advancing global health during the high-level opening session of the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly. The Director-General’s Global Leaders Awards, which were established in 2019, will go to Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot. Both recipients are distinguished scientists and public health leaders who were closely involved in the discovery of Ebola virus disease before advancing to leadership positions in global health.
translations.reference: Umqondisi Jikelele weWHO umemezele Imiklomelo yaBaholi beZempilo eMhlabeni UJean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum noPeter Piot baklonyelwe ngokuzibophezela impilo yabo yonke kwezempilo Umqondisi Jikelele weWHO uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus uzokwethula imiklomelo yempilo yonke emibili ukubona ukunikela okumangazayo ukwenza ngcono ezempilo zomhlaba ngesikhathi seseshini yokuvula esezingeni eliphakeme yoMhlangano weZempilo woMhlaba wamashumi ayisithupha nesithupha. Imiklomelo yaBaholi boMhlaba yaMqondisi Jikelele, eyasungulwa ngo-2019 izoya kuJean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum noPeter Piot. Bobabili abatholile bangosayensi abahlukile futhi bangabaholi bezempilo yomphakathi ababezibandakanye kakhulu ekutholeni isifo segciwane ibhola ngaphambi kokudlulela phambili ngesikhundla kwezobuholi kwezempilo emhlabeni.

translations.source: The award honours their lifetime achievements in public health. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is being recognized for his innovation, leadership and ground-breaking contributions to the management and treatment of diseases such as Ebola virus disease. He has played a critical role in improving health outcomes in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the African region, and the global community. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum is the Director General of the DRC’s National Institute of Biomedical Research in Kinshasa, full Professor of Microbiology at the University of Kinshasa Medical School and the inaugural president of the Congolese Academy of Science. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum was part of the team that discovered the Zaïre ebolavirus during the first recorded outbreak in 1976, in Equateur Province, Central DRC.
translations.reference: Umklomelo ubahlonipha ngabakuzuze impilo yabo yonke kwezempilo yomphakathi. USolwazi Muyembe-Tamfum waseDemocratic Republic of Congo (DRC) uyabonwa ngokwenza okusha, ubuholi bakhe nomnikelo omangazayo kuya ekuphatheni nokwelapha izifo ezinjenge sifo segciwane i-Ebola. Usedlale indima ebucayi ekwenzeni ngcono imiphumela yezempilo eDemocratic Republic of Congo, isifunda sase-Afrika kanye nomphakathi wasemhlabeni. USolwazi Muyembe-Tamfum unguMqondisi Jikelele weDRC National Institute of Biomedical Research eKinshasa, uSolwazi ogcwele weMicrobiology eNyuvesi yaseKinshasa Medical School nomongameli wokwethula waseCongolese Academy of Science. USolwazi Muyembe-Tamfum wayeyingxenye yeqembu elathola ukuthi iZaire inegciwane lebhola ngesikhathi kuqoshwa okokuqala ukubheduka ngo-1976 eSifundazweni saseEquateur, Maphakathi neDRC.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's the translation of the provided English document into Zulu.gs, aiming for accuracy and natural flow.  I've included explanations after the translation to clarify some choices.

**Zulu.gs Translation:**

"Lo mbuyiselo uyasibonisa izimpumelelo zabo zokuthola umphakathi. UProfessor Muyembe-Tamfum evela eRiphabhlikhi Demokhratike eKhongoleze (DRC) uyaziwa ngobuciko bakhe, ukukhokha kanye nezibonelo zakhe ezinkulu ekulawuleni nokuphatha izifo ezifana nesifo se-Ebola.  Kuye kwakhe umsebenzi obaluleke kakhulu ekuthuthukiseni imiphumela yezempilo eRiphabhlikhi Demokhratike eKhongoleze, esifundazweni saseAfrika, kanye nomphakathi womhlaba wonke. UProfessor Muyembe-Tamfum ngumphathi jikelele we-National Institute of Biomedical Research e-DRC eKinshasa, umfundi omuhle kakhulu we-Microbiology eNyuvesi yeKinshasa Medical School kanye nomongameli wokuqala we-Congolese Academy of Science. UProfessor Muyembe-Tamfum wayengaphakathi kwiqemba elathola i-Zaïre ebolavirus ngesikhathi sokuqala sokugcoba ngowe-1976, ePhrovinsi ye-Equateur, ekhongoleze yase-Central."

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **"Lo mbuyiselo uyasibonisa izimpumelelo zabo"**:  This translates "The award honours their lifetime achievements."  "Uyasibonisa" means "it shows us" or "it highlights."  It's a common and natural way to express the idea of an award recognizing achievements.
*   **"Riphabhlikhi Demokhratike eKhongoleze (DRC)"**:  This is the standard Zulu transliteration of "Democratic Republic of the Congo."  Keeping the acronym (DRC) in parentheses is helpful for clarity.
*   **"uyaziwa ngobuciko bakhe, ukukhokha kanye nezibonelo zakhe ezinkulu"**: This translates "is being recognized for his innovation, leadership and ground-breaking contributions."  "Ubuchiko" is innovation, "ukukhokha" is leadership, and "nezibonelo zakhe ezinkulu" means "and his great examples/contributions."
*   **"ekulawuleni nokuphatha izifo"**: This translates "in the management and treatment of diseases."
*   **"umsebenzi obaluleke kakhulu"**: This translates "played a critical role."
*   **"esifundazweni saseAfrika"**: This translates "in the African region."
*   **"umphakathi womhlaba wonke"**: This translates "the global community."
*   **"umongameli wokuqala"**: This translates "inaugural president."
*   **"ngesikhathi sokuqala sokugcoba"**: This translates "during the first recorded outbreak."
*   **"ePhrovinsi ye-Equateur"**: This is the Zulu transliteration of "Equateur Province."
*   **General Style:** I've aimed for a formal and respectful tone, appropriate for a document about an award and a distinguished professor.

**Important Notes for Zulu.gs:**

*   **Dialectal Variations:** Zulu has regional dialects. This translation aims for a standard, widely understood form.
*   **Context is Key:**  If this translation is for a specific publication or audience, further refinement might be needed to align with their preferred terminology and style.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  Ideally, a native Zulu speaker should review this translation for accuracy and naturalness.

translations.source: He later helped design one of the first effective treatments against the virus and the deployment of experimental Ebola virus disease vaccines. He has worked tirelessly to build the Democratic Republic of the Congo’s scientific capacity, establishing multiple research facilities in the country, and has played key roles with WHO in fighting infectious diseases. Professor Piot from Belgium is being recognized for his extraordinary leadership in the response to the HIV/AIDS epidemic and other significant global health challenges. He has played a vital role in launching major global health initiatives such as UNAIDS, the Global Fund, and others that have improved health outcomes around the world. Under his leadership, UNAIDS became the chief advocate for worldwide action against AIDS, also spearheading UN reform by bringing together 10 different UN organizations.
translations.reference: Makhathaleni waseza ukwakha omunye wemishanguzo youqala esebenzayo ekulweni negciwane nokufakwa kokuhlolwa kwemigomo yesifo segciwane i-Ebola. Usesebenza ngokungakhathali ukwakha iDemocratic Republic of the Congo emandleni esayensi, ukwakha izindawo zokwenza ucwaningo oluningi ezweni, usedlale indima ebaluleke kakhulu neWHO ekulweni nezifo ezithelelanayo. USolwazi Piot waseBelgium uyabonakala ngobuholi obuhle kakhulu ekuphenduleni ngobhubhane lweHIV/AIDS kanye nezinye izingqinamba ezibalulekile zezempilo emhlabeni. Usedlale indima ebaluleke kakhulu ekwethulweni kwemizamo emikhulu yezempilo emhlabeni njenge-UNAIDS, iGlobal Fund, kanye neminye esiyenze ngcono imiphumela yezempilo emhlabeni wonke. Ngaphansi kobuholi bakhe, i-UNAIDS ibe ngabameli abakhulu emhlabeni wonke bokwenza okubhekene neAIDS kanye nokukhomba indlela ekwenzeni busha i-UN ngokuhlanganisa ndawonye izinhlangano ezahlukene ezingu-10 ze-UN.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the provided English text into Zulu.gs, aiming for accuracy and natural flow.  Please read the notes at the end for important considerations about Zulu.gs and potential limitations.

**Zulu.gs Translation:**

"Kamuva lapho asiza ekwakha ukwelapha okukhulu okokuqala ngokwaphakathi ne-virus, kanye nokwethulwa kwezivakasyeli zokwelapha umdlalo we-Ebola. Uzebenze ngobuqotho ekwakheni amandla ezemfundo eRiphabhlikhi Yezindima Zomdabu ye-Congo, ekusungulweni izindawo eziningi zocwaningo ezweni, futhi udlale izindima ezibalulekile ne-WHO ekulweni nezifo eziphilayo. UProfessor Piot ovela eBelgium uyaziswa ngobunikeli balo obungajwayelekile ekuphenduleni umdlalo we-HIV/AIDS nezinhloso ezinkulu zempilo emhlabeni. Ubehlale ebanjisa indima obalulekile ekwethuleni izinhloso ezinkulu zempilo emhlabeni njenge-UNAIDS, i-Global Fund, kanye nezinye ezilungiselela imiphumela empilo emhlabeni. Phakathi kokuhamba kwakhe, i-UNAIDS yaba umuntu okhulumayo obalulekile ngokwenza umsebenzi emhlabeni ngokwelapha i-AIDS, futhi ekhuthaza ukuguqulwa kwe-UN ngokubeka 10 izinhlangano ze-UN ndawonye."

**Explanation of Choices & Key Phrases:**

*   **"Kamuva lapho..."** - "Later he..."  A common and natural way to start this sentence.
*   **"ukwelapha okukhulu okokuqala ngokwaphakathi ne-virus"** - "one of the first effective treatments against the virus."  This phrasing is designed to be clear and understandable in Zulu.
*   **"izivakasyeli zokwelapha umdlalo we-Ebola"** - "Ebola virus disease vaccines."  "Umdlalo" is used to represent "disease" in this context.
*   **"amandla ezemfundo"** - "scientific capacity."
*   **"izindawo eziningi zocwaningo"** - "multiple research facilities."
*   **"izifo eziphilayo"** - "infectious diseases."
*   **"ngobunikeli balo obungajwayelekile"** - "for his extraordinary leadership."
*   **"izinhloso ezinkulu zempilo emhlabeni"** - "significant global health challenges."
*   **"njenge-UNAIDS, i-Global Fund"** - "such as UNAIDS, the Global Fund."  Keeping the acronyms is generally acceptable.
*   **"ezilungiselela imiphumela empilo emhlabeni"** - "that have improved health outcomes around the world."
*   **"Phakathi kokuhamba kwakhe..."** - "Under his leadership..."
*   **"umuntu okhulumayo obalulekile"** - "chief advocate."
*   **"ngokwenza umsebenzi emhlabeni ngokwelapha i-AIDS"** - "for worldwide action against AIDS."
*   **"ukuguqulwa kwe-UN"** - "UN reform."
*   **"ndawonye"** - "together."

**Important Notes about Zulu.gs and Translation Considerations:**

*   **Zulu.gs is a Constructed Language:**  It's crucial to remember that Zulu.gs is *not* standard Zulu. It's a simplified, constructed language designed for easier machine translation. This means:
    *   **Grammar is Simplified:**  Zulu grammar is complex. Zulu.gs simplifies many aspects, which can sometimes lead to a loss of nuance or a slightly less elegant phrasing.
    *   **Vocabulary is Limited:** The vocabulary is smaller than standard Zulu.  I've tried to use the most appropriate words available in Zulu.gs.
    *   **Word Order:** Zulu.gs has a more rigid word order than standard Zulu.
*   **Accuracy vs. Naturalness:** I've prioritized accuracy in conveying the meaning of the original English text. However, achieving perfect naturalness in Zulu.gs is challenging due to its simplified nature.
*   **Context is Key:**  Without more context about the intended audience and purpose of the translation, it's difficult to make definitive choices about phrasing.
*   **Review by a Zulu.gs Speaker:** Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by someone fluent in Zulu.gs to ensure it is both accurate and understandable within the constraints of the language.



I hope this translation is helpful! Let me know if you have any other questions or would like me to refine it further.

translations.source: Professor Piot also played a part in the earlier discovery of the Ebola virus, and the containment of the first known outbreak, participating in field epidemiological investigations. He has chaired a number of high-level initiatives, served in numerous high-level advisory roles, including on COVID-19, and has received multiple awards. Professor Piot is the Special Advisor to the EC President, a Professor of Global Health and former Director of the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. “These outstanding individuals embody lifelong dedication, relentless advocacy, a commitment to equity, and selfless service of humanity and people’s health,” said WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “In a time of multiple emergencies, health crises, and conflict, we recognize that leadership in public health and science are more important than ever to advance global health.
translations.reference: USolwazi Piot ube yingxenye futhi yokusheshe kutholakale igciwane i-Ebola, nokulilawula ngesikhathi libheduka okokuqala lingaziwa, ukubamba iqhaza ekwenzeni ucwaningo ngezobhubhane. Usebe ngusihlalo wemizamo eminingi esezingeni eliphezulu,wasebenza emazingeni aphezulu amaningi njengomeluleki, okufaka nakuCOVID-19 usethole imiklomelo eminingi. USolwazi Piot unguMeluleki okhethekile kuMongameli wase-EC, uSolwazi weZempilo yoMhlaba owayenguMqondisi weLondon School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine. "Laba bantu abahlabahlosile banokuzinikela impilo yonke, ukumela, ukuzibophezela ekulinganeni, ukusebenzela abantu kanye nezempilo zabantu," kusho uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO, uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Ngesikhathi sokuphuthumayo okuningi,izinkinga zezempilo nokuxabana,siyabubona ubuholi kwezempilo yomphakathi nesayensi kubaluleke kakhulu ukwenzangcono ezempilo emhlabeni.
translations.hypothesis:
Here's a translation of the provided English text into Zulu.gs, aiming for accuracy and natural flow.  I've included explanations after the translation to clarify some choices and potential nuances.

**Zulu.gs Translation:**

Umfundisi Piot ubelelwe nendima ekutholweni kwesikhathi esiqalayo kwe-Ebola virus, kanye nokuvinjelwa kwesigameko sokuqala sokugqoka, abamba iqhaza ezivivinyweni ze-epidemiological ehlanganiswe emkhakheni. Uphile isihloko sezinhlelo eziningi eziphakeme, wasebenzisa izindima eziningi zokweluleka eziphakeme, kubalwa nokuthi nge-COVID-19, futhi wathola izindondo eziningi. Umfundisi Piot uyisikhulu esikhethekile somelisi we-EC, umfundisi wezempilo zomhlaba wonke, kanye nomlawuli wangaphambilaphambili we-London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. "Le mithombo emihle kakhulu iyabonakalisa ukuzithoba okukhulu impilo yonke, ukukhuthaza okungapheli, ukuzibophezele ekulinganiseni, kanye nokusebenzelana okungabhekanga kuwo ngokwabo kwabantu kanye nezempilo zabantu," kusho i-WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Ngaleso sikhathi sokugqoka okuningi, izimangaliso zezempilo, nezimpi, siyazi ukuthi ubuholi bempilo yomphakathi kanye nesayensi bunempilo ngaphezulu kunanoma yikuphi ukuthuthukisa impilo yomhlaba wonke."

translations.source: Our awardees today personify that passion.” “Their combined stories and successes show how much the world has progressed in terms of collaboration at the global, regional and country levels to strengthen health emergency preparedness, response and resilience. These outstanding scientists will continue to inspire us every day,” he added The ceremony for the awards form part of the high-level opening session of the 76th World Health Assembly being held on Sunday 21 May 2023 in Geneva.
translations.reference: Abahlonishwa bethu namhlanje bakhombisa leyo ntshisekelo." "Izindaba zabo behlangene nokuphumelela kukhombisa ukuthi umhlaba usuqhubeke kangakanani ngokokubambisana emhlabeni,esifundeni nasemazingeni ezwe ukuqinisa ukuzilungiselela okuphuthumayo kwezempilo nokuphendula nokubekezela. Amasayensisi agqamile azoqhubeka ukugqugquzela zonke izinsuku," wengezile emcimbini wemiklomelo njengengxenye yeseshini yokuvula esezingeni eliphezulu lesi-76th World Health Assembly ozobanjwa ngesonto mhlaka-21 May 2023 eGeneva.

translations.source: WHO congratulates Benin and Mali for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem WHO has validated Benin and Mali as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in WHO’s African Region to achieve this significant milestone. Countries that previously received WHO validation for trachoma elimination are Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022) and Malawi (September 2022). “WHO congratulates the health authorities of Benin and Mali and their network of global and local partners for these milestones”, said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Following Benin’s and Mali’s success, trachoma remains endemic in 23 countries in WHO’s African Region, bringing us a step closer towards the elimination target for trachoma set in the road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030.”
translations.reference: I-WHO ihalalisela iBenin neMali ngokuqeda isifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi I-WHO isiqinisekise iBenin neMali ngokuqda isifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi,nokubenza abesihlanu nesithupha amazwe aseAfrika eWHO ukuthola umsebenzi omkhulu kangaka. Amazwe asethole ukugunyazwa okuvela kwiWHO ngokuqeda isifo samehlo yiGhana (ngoNhlangulana 2018) iGambia (ngoMbasa 2021) Togo (ngoNhlaba 2022 kanye neMalawi (ngoMandulo 2022). "WHO ihalalisela abasemagunyeni bezempilo baseBenin neMali kanye nohlelokuxhumana lwabo emhlabeni kanye nababambisani bendawo kulokhu asebekuzuzile," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Kulandela impumelelo yeBenin neMali isifo samehlo siyaqhubeka siba wubhubhane emazweni angu-23 eWHO African Region, kusiletha eduze kokuqedwa okuhlosiwe okuhleliwe kwesifo samehlo kubalazwe kulezofifo ezijwaelekile ezinganakiwe 2021-2030."

Izwe elangaphambilaphambili elathola ukuqinisekisa kwe-WHO ngokukhipha umdlalo we-trachoma yi-Ghana (June 2018), i-Gambia (April 2021), i-Togo (May 2022) kanye ne-Malawi (September 2022). "I-WHO ibongela izinhloko zezempilo ze-Benin ne-Mali kanye nenethiwekhi yabo yabalingani bezehlukahlukene emhlabeni nendawo nala mkhuba," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, i-Director-General ye-WHO. translations.source: Globally, Benin and Mali join 15 other countries that have been validated by WHO for having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These are Cambodia, China, the Gambia, Ghana, Islamic Republic of Iran, Lao People’s Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo and Vanuatu. Trachoma elimination using WHO’s SAFE strategy Both Benin and Mali implemented the WHO-recommended SAFE strategy to eliminate trachoma with the support of WHO and partners. The SAFE strategy consists of surgery to treat late trachoma complications; antibiotics to clear infection; facial cleanliness; and environmental improvement, particularly improving access to water and sanitation, to reduce transmission.
translations.reference: Emhlabeni iBenin neMali bazojoyina amanye amazwe angu-15 asegunyazwe yiWHO ngokuqeda isifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi. Lawo mazwe yiCambodia, iChina, iGambia, iGhana, i-Islamic Republic of Iran, iLao Peoples Democratic Republic, iMalawi, iMexico, iMorocco, iMyanmar, iNepal, i-Oman, iSaudi Arabia, iTogo neVanuatu. Ukuqeda isifo samehlo usebenzisa isu leSAFE leWHO Womabili amazwe iBenin neMali benze isiphakamiso sesu lokuphepha leWHO ukuqeda isifo samehlo ngokwesekwa yiWHO nababambisani. Isu leSAFE liqukethe ukuhlinzwa ukuze welaphe isifo samehlo ngobunzia,ama-antibhayothikhi ukuqeda ukungcola,izinto zokuhlanza ubuso,ukwenzangcono indawo,ikakhulukazi ukwenza ngcono ukuthola amazi nokuthuthwa kwendle ukwehlisa ukudlulisela.

translations.source: Through the International Trachoma Initiative, the antibiotic azithromycin is donated by Pfizer to elimination programmes implementing the SAFE strategy. Benin has integrated trachoma elimination interventions with those implemented against other neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), under the umbrella of the National Programme for Communicable Diseases. Trachoma is the third NTD to be eliminated in Benin, after dracunculiasis (in 2009) and gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (in 2021). Mali has conducted trachoma impact and surveillance surveys and rolled out interventions to achieve elimination targets, despite security challenges in the northern regions of the country and sociopolitical upheavals in recent years. Trachoma is the first NTD to be eliminated in Mali, which therefore now joins a global group of 47 countries that have eliminated at least one NTD.
translations.reference: Ngomzamo wesifo samehlo wamazwe angaphandle,antibhayothikhi azithromycin xhase ngePfizer ekuqedweni kwezinhlelo zokwenza zesu leSAFE. I-Benin isihlanganise isifo samehlo ukuqedwa nokungenelela kulabo abaenza okubhekene nezinye izifo ezijwayelekile ezinganakiwe (amaNTD) ngaphansi kwesambulela seNationa Programme for Communicable Disesases. Isifo samehlo ngesesithathu kuNTD ukuqedwa eBenin emva kwedracunculiasis (ngo-2009) negambiese yabantu eAfrika trypanosomiasis (ngo-2021). Imali isicwaninge umthelela wesifo samehlo nocwaningo lokubheka nokukhipha izingenelelo ukuthola okuqondwe ukuqedwa,ngaphandle kwezingqinamba zokuphepha ezifundeni zasenyakatho yezwe nezinkinga zepolitiki yezenhlalakahle eminyakeni yakamuva nje. Isifo samehlo ngesokuqala seNTD ukuqedwa eMali ngakho manje esijoyina iqoqo lomhlaba lamazwe angu-47 aseqede okungenani esisodwa seNTD.

Nge-International Trachoma Initiative, i-antibiotic ethi azithromycin inikezelwa yi-Pfizer kwi-program zokukhipha igama le-trachoma ezisebenzisa i-SAFE strategy. I-Benin yenze ukuhlanganisa ukwelashwa kwegama le-trachoma nokwelashwa kwezifo zabanye abantu abangaphethwanga (NTDs), ngaphansi kwe-National Programme for Communicable Diseases. Igama le-trachoma liyigama lesithathu le-NTD elikhiphwe e-Benin, ngemva kwe-dracunculiasis (ngo-2009) kanye ne-gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (ngo-2021). I-Mali yenze izivivinyo zokuhlola umphumela we-trachoma nokugada nokwethula izindlela zokwelashwa ukuze kufinyelelwe kokuphothulwa kwemigomo, noma kunjalo, kunazo izinkinga zokuphepha ezindaweni zasenyakatho lezwe kanye nokuphazanyiswa kwemvelo yangomuntu ngeminyaka yamuva. Igama le-trachoma liyigama lokuqala le-NTD elikhiphwe e-Mali, ngakho manje linama-47 amazwe omhlaba asekhiphe amathathu NTDs amathathu.

translations.source: “These are impressive public health achievements,” said Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall, Director of the WHO Global NTD Programme. “Benin and Mali demonstrate how strong political will, cross-sector integration, surveillance and community engagement can work in concert to achieve disease elimination.” Significant progress has been made in the fight against trachoma over the past few years. The number of people requiring antibiotic treatment for trachoma in the WHO African Region fell by 84 million, from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million as of June 2022. Note to the editor: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022) with an estimated 125 million people living in areas requiring interventions against the disease.
translations.reference: "Lokhu ukuzuza kwezempilo yomphakathi okujabulisayo," kusho uDkt Ibrahima Soce Fall uMqondisi woHlelo lweNTD eMhlabeni lweWHO. Ukuqhubeka okubalulekile sekwenzekile ekulweni okubhekene nesifo samehlo eminyakeni embalwa edlule. Abantu abaningi badinga kwelashwa nge antibhayothikhi ukuqeda isifo samehlo eWHO Afrikan Region abafika ezigidini ezingu-84 kusuka kuzigidi ezingu-189 ngo-2014 kuya kuzigidi ezingu-105 kusuka ngoNhlangulana 2022. Inothi eliya kumhleli: Isifo samehlo (Trachoma) sizohlala siyinkinga yezempilo yomphakathi emazweni angu-41 (kusuka ngoNhlangulana 2022) ngesibalo esilinganiselwe kuzigidi ezingu-125 zabantu abaphila ezindaweni ezidinga ukungenelela okubhekene nalesifo.

translations.source: Trachoma is found mainly in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western Pacific and the Middle East. The WHO African Region is disproportionately affected by trachoma with 105 million people living in at-risk areas, which represents 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person.
translations.reference: Isifo samehlo sitholakala kakhulu ezindaweni ezingamakhaya futhi ezihluphekayo e-Afrika, Maphakathi naseNingizimu neAmerica, Asia naseWestern Pacific kanye neMiddle East. I-WHO African Region itheleleke yisifo samehlo kubantu abayizigidi ezingu 105 abahlala ezindaweni eziyingcuphe,ezimele u-48% womthwalo wesifo samehlo emhlabeni. Isifo samehlo isona esihamba phambili ekuthelelaneni esidala ukungaboni emhlabeni wonke. Isifo sidalwa ukuthelelana ngegciwane iChlamydia trachomatis. Ukuthelelana kudluliseka kumuntu kuye kumuntu ngeminwe engcolile, izinambuzane, izimpukane esezihlangabezane nokungcola okuphuma emahlweni noma ikhala lomuntu othelelekile.

translations.source: Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, inadequate access to water or use of proper sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020). With other partners in the Alliance, WHO supports country implementation of the SAFE strategy and strengthening of national capacity through epidemiological assessment, monitoring, surveillance, project evaluation and resource mobilization contributing towards elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The road map, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its decision 73(33), sets 2030 as the new target date for global elimination.
translations.reference: Izinkinga zengcephe yendawo yokudluliselwa kwesifo samehlo okufaka ukungahlanzeki,imizi egcwele ukungatholi kahle amanzi nokusebenzisa izindawo zokuzikhulula ezifanele. Ngo-1996, iWHO yethula iWHO Alliane yokuqedwa kwesifo samehlo emhlabeni ngonyaka ka-2020 (GET2020). Nabanye ababambisani kwi-Alliance, iWHO yeseka ukwenza kwezwe kweSu lokuPhepha nokuqinisa amandla ezwe ngokhlolwa kwezobhubhane, ukubheka, ukuhlola, ukubhekisa iphrojekthi nokuhlanganisa izinsizakusebenza ezizosiza ekuqedweni kwesifo samehlo njengenkinga yezempilo yomphakathi. Umgwaqo balazwe ogunyazwe nguMhlangano weZempilo eMhlabeni ngo-2020 ngesinqumo sawo 73(33) ibeka u-2030 njengosuku oluqondiwe olusha lokuqeda emhlabeni.

translations.source: 152 million babies born preterm in the last decade Of every 10 babies born, 1 is preterm – and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies Preterm birth rates have not changed in the past decade in any region of the world The impacts of conflict, climate change, COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere Action is needed urgently to improve the prevention of preterm birth, alongside better care for affected babies and their families

translations.source: An estimated 13.4 million babies were born pre-term in 2020, with nearly 1 million dying from preterm complications, according to a new report released by United Nations agencies and partners today. This is equivalent to around 1 in 10 babies born early (before 37 weeks of pregnancy) worldwide. Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020.
translations.reference: Isilinganiso sezigindi ezingu-13.4 izingane ezizalwe ngaphambi kwesikhathi nokungafika esigidini esisodwa semithelela yokufa, ngokombiko omusha okhishiwe yi-United Nations ama-ejensi kanye nababambisani namhlanje. Lo mzuliswano wokulingana wengane eyodwa kweziyishumi ezisheshe zizalwe (ngaphambi kwamasonto angu-37 okukhulelwa) emhlabeni wonke. Ukusheshe uzalwe: ishuminyaka lokwenza ekuzalweni ngaohambi kwesikhathi, kukhiqizwe Inhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni (WHO) ne United Nations Children Fund (UNICEF) ndawonye nePMNCH -- ubudlelwane obukhulu emhlabeni babantu besifazane, izingane, nentsha, kuveza ukuuthusa bokuthula kokuphuthumayo kwezokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi, okunesikhathi eside kunganakiwe esikalini sakho sobucayi okuveza ukuqhubeka ekwenzeningcono ezempilo zezingane nokuphila. Umbiko ukubuyekezwa kulinganisa okuphuma kuWHO ne-UNICEF, izilungiselele neLondon School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, ekusabalaleni kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi. Kukhona konke kutholakala ukuthi amazinga okuzalwa kwezingane ngaphambi kwesikhathi awakashintshi kunoma yisiphi isifunda emhlabeni kuleshuminyaka eledlule nezigidi ezingu-152 zezingane ezihluphekile ezisheshe zazalwa kusuka ngo-2010 kuya ku-2020.
translations.hypothesis:

**Zulu.gs Translation:**

"Kusho ukuthi cishe imiliona e-13.4 yezingane zizalelwe ngaphambi kwesikhathi ngonyaka ka-2020, futhi cishe imiliona eyodwa yashona ngenxa yezinkinga ezihlobene nokuselahla kwaphambilini, njengoba kubikwa kulo mhloliso omusha owethulwe amaphakheji e-United Nations kanye nabalingani bayo namuhla. Lokhu lufanela cishe ku-1 kwezingu-10 izingane ezazalelwe ngaphambilini (ngaphambi kweziviki ezingu-37 zokukhulelwa) emhlabeni wonke. "Kuselahla Kwaphambilini: Isikhathi Sokwenza Izinto" (Born Too Soon: Decade of Action on Preterm Birth), elikhiqizwe yi-World Health Organization (WHO) kanye ne-United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) ngokubambisana ne-PMNCH – umbuto omkhulu emhlabeni wezinsukuzonke wezinsukwana, izingane, kanye nabadadeki, ukhipha isigxivizo ngokuthi "ngososomane obimhlophe" lokuselahla kwaphambilini, elingakakwaziwa kahle isikhathi eside ngokubalwa kanye nobunzima, okuthintela ukuthuthukisa ukuthuthukisa impilo nezimpilo zabantwa.  Lomhloliso uqukethe izibalo ezibuyekezwe zi-WHO kanye ne-UNICEF, ezilungiselewe ngokubambisana ne-London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, ngokuthi zingaki izinkinga zokuzalelwa ngaphambilini.  Sekugijima, uthola ukuthi amanani okuselahla kwaphambilini awashintshi kunoma yiziphi izifunda emhlabeni phakathi neminyaka yezinkulungwane eziyishumi, ngokusho kwezinsukwana ezingu-152 ezilambile ezazalelwe ngaphambilini phakathi konyaka ka-2010 kuya ku-2020."

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations for Zulu.gs:**

*   **Zulu.gs Specifics:** Zulu.gs is a simplified version of Zulu, designed for easier comprehension and wider accessibility. This translation prioritizes clarity and avoids overly complex grammatical structures.
*   **Numbers:** I've used the Zulu word for "million" (imiliona) and kept the numerical values as they are, as this is common practice in Zulu.gs.

    *   "Pre-term" is translated as "ngaphambilini" (before time).
    *   "Decade of action" is translated as "Isikhathi Sokwenza Izinto" (Time to do things).
*   **"Silent Emergency":** This is a powerful metaphor. I translated it as "ngososomane obimhlophe" (a white silence), which captures the idea of something hidden and dangerous.
*   **Word Order:** Zulu sentence structure can be flexible. I've aimed for a natural flow while maintaining grammatical correctness.
*   **Formal vs. Informal:** The document is formal, so the translation maintains a formal tone.
*   **Abbreviations:** I've kept the abbreviations (WHO, UNICEF, PMNCH) as they are, as they are often recognized internationally.
*   **Clarity over Literal Translation:** In some cases, a literal translation would be awkward or confusing in Zulu.gs. I've prioritized conveying the meaning accurately, even if it means deviating slightly from the original wording.

translations.source: Preterm birth is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 of all deaths of children occurring before their 5th birthday. Preterm survivors can face lifelong health consequences, with an increased likelihood of disability and developmental delays. Survival gaps by region, income, race Building from a landmark report on the topic in 2012, this new Born too soon “decade” report provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its profound impact on women, families, societies and economies. Too often, where babies are born determines if they survive.
translations.reference: Ukuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi manje sekuyimbangela eholayo ekufeni kwezingane,ukubala okungaphezu kuka 1 wokungu 5 wakho konke ukufa okwenzeka ngaphambi kosuku lokuzalwa lweminyaka emihlanu. Abaphilayo kwabazalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi babhekana nemiphumela yezempilo impilo yabo yonke, ngokukhula kokujwayeleka kokukhubazeka kanye nokubambezeleka kokukhula. Izikhala zokuphila yisifunda, imali engenayo, ibala Ukwakhela emmbikweni wesikhonkwane esihlokweni sango-2012,lokhu kusheshe uzalwe, ishuminyaka, umbiko unikeza ukubhekwa okukhulu kokusabalala kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi nomthelela oqinile kwabesifazane, imiphakathi nezomnotho. Kujwayelekile lapho izingane zizalelwe khona kuzonquma ukuthi zizophila yini.
translations.hypothesis:

translations.source: The report notes that only 1 in 10 extremely preterm babies (<28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries. Gaping inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality care determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries. Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm birth, and preterm babies in these regions face the highest mortality risk. Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of preterm births globally. The report also highlights that the impacts of conflict, climate change and environmental damage, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere.
translations.reference: Umbiko uqaphela ukuthi uyedwa kuphela kwabangu-10 abantwana abasheshe bazalwa (<amasonto angu-28) abaphilayo emzaweni anemali encane engenayo,uma uqhathanisa kwangaphezu kwangu-9 ku-10 emazweni anemali ephezulu engenayo. Izikhala zokungalingani ezihlobene nebala, nobuzwe nemali engenayo nokthola ukunakekelwa okufanele kukhomba ukuphila kokusheshe uzalwe ukufa kanye nokukhubazeka ngisho nasemazweni anemali ephezulu engenayo. Southern Asia nesub-Saharan Afrika banamazinga aphezulu okusheshe kuzalwe kulezizizfunda futhi abhekene nengcuphe yokushona kwezingane ephezulu. Ndawonye, lezi zifunda ezimbili zibala ngaphezu kuka-65% wezingane ezisheshe zazalwa emhlabeni. Umbiko uphinde uveze umthelela wengxabano,ukuguquka kwesimo sezulu nokulimala kwendawo, COVID-19 nokunyuka kokubiza kwempilo okunyusa ingcuphe kubantu besifazane nezingane yonke indawo.

translations.source: For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million preterm births each year. Nearly 1 in 10 preterm babies are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises, according to a new analysis in the report. Maternal health risks, such as adolescent pregnancy and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to preterm births. This underlines the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including effective family planning, with high-quality care in pregnancy and around the time of birth. Agenda for action: more country investment and parent-led activism
translations.reference: Isibonelo, ukungcola komoya kulinganiselwa ukuthi kunomthelela wezigidi ezingu-6 zokusheshe kuzalwe unyaka ngamunye. Kungafika ku 1 kwabangu 10 izingane ezisheshe zazalwa ezizalwe emazweni abuthaka angu-10 akhahlamezwe izinkinga zabantu ngokokuhlaziya okusha embikweni. Ingcuphe yomama abakhulelwe, njengokukhulelwa kwamatshitshi kusondelene nokusheshe uzale. Lokhu kugqamisa isidingo sokuqinisekisa ukuthola ezempilo zokuzala nezocansi, okufaka ukusebenza kokuhlela umndeni, ukunakekelwa okusezingeni eliphezulu kwabakhulelwe nangesikhathi sokuzala. Ajenda yokwenza: utshalomali oluningi ezweni nokushoshova okuholwa ngabazali

Izinkinga zezempilo zabanye abavikela, njengokukhathazeka kwabasha nokukhubazeka kwegazi, zihlobane kakhulu nokuzalelwa ngaphambilapha. Lokhu kubonisa ukuthi kufanele siqinisekise ukufinyelela kulezi zinsizakalo zezempilo zombili nezokuzalisa, kubandakanya ukuhlelwa kahle komndeni, kanye nokunakekelwa okukhulu ngesifazandali kanye lapho umntu ezalelwa khona. Uhlelo lokwenza izinto zisebenze: ukwethembeka kwemali ngaphakathi kwezwe kanye nokuzimela kwabazali."

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Ukuqhambisa" (For example):** A standard and clear way to introduce an example.

*   **"Amazweni ayi-10 asenkingeni kakhulu, ayingozi, ahlonzeleke ngobunzima bezezimali" (The 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises):** This is a longer phrase to accurately convey the meaning.  I've used a combination of terms:
    *   "Asenkingeni kakhulu" - "are in great difficulty/crisis"

translations.source: The past decade has also seen a growth of community activism on preterm birth and stillbirth prevention, driven by networks of parents, health professionals, academia, civil society, and others. Throughout the world, groups for affected families of preterm birth have been at the forefront of advocating for access to better care and policy change and supporting other families. Leading up to the International Maternal Newborn Health Conference, in Cape Town, South Africa from 8-11 May, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA and PMNCH are calling for the following actions to improve care for women and newborns and mitigate risks from preterm births: Increased investments: Mobilizing international and domestic resources to optimize maternal and newborn health, ensuring high quality care is provided when and where it is needed. Accelerated implementation: Meeting country targets for progress through implementing established national policies for maternal and newborn care.
translations.reference: Ishuminyaka eledlulesibone ukukhula kwezishoshovu emphakathini ekuvinjweni kokusheshe kuzalwe kwezingane, okuholwa uhlelokuxhumana lwabazali, izifundiswa zempilo, izifundiswa umphakathi nabanye. Ezweni lonke amaqoqo emindeni ekhahlamezekile ngokusheshe kuzalwe kwengane alokhu ephambili ekumeleni ukuthola ukunakekelwa okungcono nokushinthwa kwenqubomgomo nokweseka imindeni. Okuholela kuInternational Maternal Newborn Helath Conference, eKapa eNingizimu Afrika kusuka ngo-8-11 kuNhlaba, iWHO, i-UNICEF, i-UNFPA nePMNCH bamemezela ukulandela izenzo ukwenza ngcono ukunakekela kwabesifazane nabazelwe ubukhulu bezingcuphe okuvela kokuzalwa ngaphambi kwesikhathi. Ukunyuswa kotshalomali:ukuhlanganisa izinsizakusebenza zamazwe angaphandle nezendawo ukusiza ezempilo zomama nezingane ezizelwe, ukuqinisekisa ukunakekelwa okuphezulu kuyanikezwa uma noma nalapho kudingeka khona. Ukunyuswa kokwenza: Ukuhlangabezana nokuqondwe ngamazwe kokuqhubeka ngokwenza okusungulwe yinqubomgomo yezwe kokunakekelwa komama nezingane ezizelwe.

translations.source: Integration across sectors: Promoting education through the life cycle; supporting smarter economic investments, with co-financing across sectors; strengthening climate adaptation responses across the life-course; and advancing the coordination and resilience of emergency systems. Locally driven innovation: Investing in locally led innovation and research to support improvements in quality of care and equity in access. Quotes: Prof. Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine Co-lead, Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023) and Born too soon (2012) "This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the last decade was 152 million babies born too soon.
translations.reference: Ukuhlanganisa kuyona yonke imikhakha: Ukugqugquzela ezemfundo ngempilo, ukweseka utshalomali lwezomnotho oluhlakaniphile, nokubambisana kwezimali kuyona yonke imikhakha, ukuqinisa ukuhambisana kwesimo sezulu nokuphendula kuyona yonke impilo, ukwenza ngcono ukuxhumana nokubekezela ezinhlelweni eziphuthumayo. Okwenziwa abendawo okusha: ukutshala endaweni okuholwa okusha nocwaningo lokweseka intuthuko yokunakekelwa okuhle nokuthola ngokulingana. Okucashuniwe: uSolwazi Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine Omunye wabaholayoUsheshe wazalwa-ishuminyaka lwesenzo ekushesheni uzalwe (2023) noKusheshe uzalwe (2012) "Lombiko omusha ukhombisa ukuthi ukubiza kokungenzi kulelishuminyaka eledlule kwakuyizigidi ezingu-152 ezisheshe zazalwa.

translations.source: While some regions are more affected, preterm birth threatens health progress in every country. Greater investment in the care of vulnerable newborns can save millions of families from heartbreak. More work is also needed to prevent preterm birth, which will also improve progress in reducing stillbirths and maternal deaths. Together these twin tracks of preterm prevention and care will produce healthier individuals and societies to deliver on economic and social development. Our next generation depends on us all acting now -- the investment may not be small but the returns on this investment will be major for every country"
translations.reference: Ngesikhathi kwezinye izifunda bekhahlamezeke kakhulu ukusheshe uzale kusabisa ukuqhubeka kwezempilo kuwona wonke amazwe. Utahlomali olukhulu ekunakekelweni kwabazelwe ababuthaka kungasindisa izigidi zemindeni ekuphukeni kwenhliziyo. Umsebenzi omuningi uyadingeka ukugwema ukusheshe kuzalwe, okuzophinde kwenze ngcono ukuqhubeka kokwehlisa ukufa komama nezingane ezisheshe zazalwa. Ndawonye lokhu okubili kokuvikela ukusheshe kuzalwe nokunakekelwa kuzokhiqiza abantu abanempilo nemiphakathi ukuletha kwezomnotho kanye nokuthuthukiswa kwenhlalakahle. Isizukulwane sethu esizayo sincike kuthina kukhona konke esikwenzayo manje-utshalomali ngeke lube luncane kodwa izimbuyiselo kulolutshalomali luzoba lukhulu kakhulu wonke amazwe"
translations.hypothesis:
